slides.eyebrow: Behind the Scenes · Anatomy of a sync brief
slides.headline: The brief said:
"90 seconds.
slides.lede: Orchestral. Afrofusion influence. Hopeful. No vocals. One-stop only." · Every word in a sync brief is a specification. Not a suggestion. ↓

slides.headline:
🎻 ORCHESTRAL
Real strings or a convincing orchestral arrangement.
slides.lede: Bedroom synth pads don't make the shortlist here.

slides.headline:
🌍 AFROFUSION INFLUENCE
The tonal and rhythmic language of African music — not pastiche, not performed.

😌 HOPEFUL
Not happy.
slides.lede: Not triumphant. Hopeful is quieter, more complex. The distinction matters enormously to the picture edit.

slides.headline:
🚫 NO VOCALS
Not even background vocals.
slides.lede: Not even a hum. Fully instrumental or the track is out.

slides.headline:
✅ ONE-STOP ONLY
You control both master and publishing.
slides.lede: If you need a label involved, the track is disqualified.

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: Where sync briefs come from and who writes them · Why duration is always absolute · How to read instrumentation and influence language · Mood vocabulary — the most misread field in any brief · Save this for when your first brief arrives.

brief_card.title: Afro-fusion for luxury lifestyle ad — global campaign
brief_card.notes: 30-second hero spot + 15-second cut. Must be stems-ready.
